title: Programmers and software development professionals
description: Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. Analyze science, engineering, business, and other data processing problems to develop and implement solutions to complex applications problems, system administration issues, or network concerns. Perform systems management and integration functions, improve existing computer systems, and review computer system capabilities, workflow, and schedule limitations. May analyze or recommend commercially available software. Apply knowledge of nursing and informatics to assist in the design, development, and ongoing modification of computerized health care systems. May educate staff and assist in problem solving to promote the implementation of the health care system. (Based on 29 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Develop websites and employ web design standards to ensure usability and functionality.
Conduct trial runs of programs and software applications to ensure correct instructions and outputs.
Train subordinates and users in programming, coding, and the use of computer systems.
Assign, coordinate, and review the work and activities of programming personnel to ensure project objectives are met.
Consult with computer operators, system analysts, and users to define and resolve issues in running computer programs.
Perform systems analysis, maintain or control the use of computer systems software, and identify hardware and software solutions.
Prepare detailed workflow charts and diagrams that describe processes and convert them into coded instructions in a computer language.
Write, analyze, review, and update computer programs or software packages to meet specific operational needs.
Compile documentation of program development and revisions, including comments for user understanding.
Correct errors in programs through testing and make necessary modifications to ensure expected outcomes.
Define system goals and devise flow charts describing the operational steps needed for software implementation.
Analyze and interpret user needs and software requirements to ensure designs meet time and cost constraints.
Confer with systems analysts, engineers, and other stakeholders to design systems and understand project limitations and capabilities.
Monitor and analyze system performance, and propose recommendations for improvements to enhance efficiency and usability.
Develop security measures to protect information in computer systems against unauthorized access or data loss.
Engage in multidisciplinary projects, utilizing emerging technologies to solve complex computing problems.
Provide technical support and guidance to junior staff or clients for system or programming related issues.
Estimate costs for system implementation and operation, preparing requisitions for necessary hardware and software.
Implement training programs and manuals for new software or systems adopted by the organization.
Review product specifications and prepare reports on project activities and statuses for management evaluation.
Design and implement prevention, maintenance, backup, or recovery procedures to ensure system reliability.
Develop data models and ensure alignment of system performance standards with business objectives.
Implement controls to provide security for operating systems, software, and data.
Document user support activity, such as system problems, corrective actions, and equipment installations.
Communicate with vendors to gather product information, resolve problems, or address system maintenance issues.
Evaluate and maintain networks by monitoring performance, performing backups, and making adjustments based on capacity needs.
Prepare project schedules, budgets, and specifications, while coordinating installation and maintenance activities.
Research and test new hardware or software products to determine performance and interoperability requirements.
Diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve hardware, software, or network problems, and replace defective components as needed.
Train users in computer system functionalities and assist in problem-solving for operational issues.
Develop and recommend network security measures, including firewalls and audits, to protect data and hardware.
Create web site specifications, including product features and technical requirements, while ensuring compliance with standards.
Design, build, or maintain web sites using authoring languages, along with integrating solutions and features per customer needs.
Collaborate with teams to evaluate software performance, usability, and functionality, providing feedback to developers as necessary.
Document testing procedures and results to ensure replicability and compliance with industry standards.
Coordinate user testing and evaluate customer problems to recommend solutions and troubleshoot issues.
Develop and implement procedures for ongoing web site revisions and updates to meet user needs and technology changes.
Maintain and administer computer networks and systems software to ensure efficient operations and performance.
Conduct historical analyses of test results and design test plans or procedures for software performance evaluations.
Install and configure software and hardware to support web applications, ensuring they meet operational requirements.
Document technical factors such as server load, bandwidth, and database performance.
Direct pre-production activities, like creating moodboards or establishing project timelines.
Develop, validate, and document test routines and schedules to ensure test cases address all browser and device types.
Develop Web site maps, application models, and templates that meet project goals or industry standards.
Write and edit technical documentation for digital interface products like user manuals and testing protocols.
Design, build, or maintain Web sites using authoring or scripting languages and content creation tools.
Collaborate with development teams to discuss, analyze, or resolve usability issues in applications.
Test programs, correct errors, and make necessary modifications to improve application performance.
Document, design, code, or test applications using Geographic Information Systems (GIS) or mapping solutions.
Coordinate documentation of security policies and procedures and monitor networks for security breaches.
Negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain resources or materials.
Collaborate with Web developers to create and manage internal and external Web sites or manage projects like e-marketing campaigns.
Develop data warehouse process models, including sourcing, loading, transformation, and extraction, and implement data extraction procedures from other systems.
Monitor or track project milestones and deliverables, providing feedback to project team members and documenting their performance.
Schedule and facilitate meetings related to information technology projects and establish and execute a project communication plan.
Assess current or future customer needs by communicating directly, conducting surveys, or other methods to improve project outcomes.
Confer with project personnel to identify and resolve problems and develop effective response strategies through risk assessments.
Prepare project status reports by collecting, analyzing, and summarizing project information and trends.
Develop and manage work breakdown structure (WBS) of information technology projects including objectives, technologies, and schedules.
Direct the analysis, development, and operation of complete computer systems, including evaluating and implementing system architecture.
Create plans, test files, and scripts for data warehouse testing, ensuring quality through the creation of supporting documentation.
Develop and implement business rules via stored procedures, middleware, or other technologies to enhance data warehouse efficiency.
Develop, document, or maintain standards, best practices, or system usage procedures for data warehousing and system architecture.
Verify the structure, accuracy, or quality of warehouse data while identifying and correcting deviations from standards.
Develop or maintain standards for the design of data warehouse elements, including data architectures and models.
Provide technical support and guidance to junior staff or clients and assist with troubleshooting system issues.
Document installation or configuration procedures and prepare functional or technical documentation for data warehouses.
Monitor system operation to detect potential problems, ensuring stability, interoperability, and security of IT systems.
Evaluate testing routines for adequacy and assess existing systems to determine effectiveness and suggest improvements.
Provide or coordinate troubleshooting support for data warehouses while ensuring adherence to quality standards in project deliverables.
Document technical functions and specifications for new or proposed content management systems.
